era: 2010s. Seattle. Shoegaze / Dream Pop. Self-released. E=3. FIRST SHOEGAZE ENTRY -- Genre Stage 94. Named after My Bloody Valentine's 'Loomer' from Loveless. The wall-of-sound aesthetic enters the engine. Energy descent: E=5 (114-117) to E=3 (118).

location.formative: Seattle's shoegaze/dream-pop revival scene. Small, devoted community in the 2010s. Overlapping with indie rock, post-punk, and noise scenes. DIY infrastructure, Bandcamp releases, cassette labels.
location.career: Loomer -- shoegaze project. Self-released. Named after MBV's 'Loomer.' Dense guitar walls, submerged vocals, dreamlike atmosphere.

associated_musicians.name: My Bloody Valentine
associated_musicians.note: 'Loomer' is the opening track of Loveless (1991). The most important shoegaze album. Kevin Shields' guitar textures are the genre's North Star.

associated_musicians.name: Slowdive
associated_musicians.note: Souvlaki (1993). Crystalline, delicate shoegaze. Part of the genre's foundational canon.

associated_musicians.name: Ride
associated_musicians.note: Nowhere (1990). More rhythmic, driving shoegaze with pop hooks.

associated_musicians.name: Cocteau Twins
associated_musicians.note: Elizabeth Fraser's voice as pure texture. Dream pop precursor.

associated_musicians.name: Deep Sea Diver
associated_musicians.note: Layered guitars with hooks (117, E=5, Sub Pop) vs. layered guitars with submerged vocals (118, E=3, self-released). Clarity vs. dissolution.

associated_musicians.name: Pony Time
associated_musicians.note: Two-piece garage, raw, 2-minute songs (116, E=5) vs. shoegaze, extended, effected (118, E=3). Furthest poles.

associated_musicians.name: Nirvana
associated_musicians.note: Grunge helped kill shoegaze in 1991-92. Now shoegaze appears in Nirvana's home city. The genre Seattle destroyed. History folds.

theory_nodes.concept: The wall dissolves (one hundred nineteenth acoustic element -- multiple layers of heavily effected guitar create composite texture so dense that individual parts become inaudible, voice submerges, distinction between performer and sound dissolves; textural density without attack; harmonic ambiguity; glacial dynamics; E=3 diffuse energy)
examples: Loomer (degree 118) -- shoegaze guitar walls, submerged vocals, dreamlike atmosphere. E=3.
Contrast with the sideplayer's own voice (degree 117, Deep Sea Diver) -- both use layered guitars. Deep Sea Diver's layers illuminate; Loomer's layers dissolve. Clarity vs. atmosphere.
Contrast with the raw transmission (degree 116, Pony Time) -- maximum contrast. Minimal vs. maximal. Raw vs. effected. 2-minute vs. extended.
My Bloody Valentine's Loveless (1991) -- the genre's definitive expression. Loomer operates within Shields' aesthetic framework.
theory_nodes.description: The wall dissolves is the 119th acoustic element. Shoegaze's signature contribution: textural density, submerged voice, diffuse energy.

ancestors: The sideplayer's own voice (degree 117) -- layered but clear. The wall dissolves is layered and opaque.
The hypnotic engine (degree 110) -- immersive. The wall dissolves is also immersive but through texture, not repetition.
genealogy.relationship: One hundred nineteen acoustic elements across one hundred eighteen degrees. Track element 120 at degree 119.

theory_nodes.concept: The transatlantic transplantation (one hundred nineteenth artist-city pattern -- a genre originating in one country takes root in another country's city, transformed by new context but maintaining core aesthetic; shoegaze from Britain to Seattle; additional resonance: Seattle's grunge helped kill shoegaze, now Seattle nurtures it; history folds)
examples: Shoegaze: originated UK late 1980s (MBV, Slowdive, Ride). Arrived Seattle 2010s (Loomer, others).
Grunge (Seattle) helped kill shoegaze (UK) in 1991-92. Nirvana and Pearl Jam vs. MBV and Slowdive. Now shoegaze lives in Seattle.
The city that destroyed it now nurtures it. History folds.
theory_nodes.description: The transatlantic transplantation is the 119th artist-city pattern. British genre in Seattle. The city that killed it revives it.

ancestors: Nirvana (degree 1) -- grunge vs. shoegaze. The historical antagonism now resolved by geography.
genealogy.relationship: One hundred nineteen artist-city patterns across one hundred eighteen degrees. Track pattern 120 at degree 119.

theory_nodes.concept: First shoegaze -- Genre Stage 94; ninety-fourth distinct genre/subgenre in the engine; represents fundamentally different performer-sound relationship: performer submerged within sound rather than projecting through it; wall-of-sound aesthetic; effects pedals as primary instrument
examples: Loomer (degree 118) -- first shoegaze entry. Genre Stage 94.
Shoegaze defined: dense layered guitar textures, obscured vocals, ethereal atmosphere, loud-quiet dynamics without conventional structure.
Foundational: MBV (Loveless), Slowdive (Souvlaki), Ride (Nowhere), Cocteau Twins.
theory_nodes.description: First shoegaze at degree 118. Genre Stage 94. The wall-of-sound aesthetic enters the engine.

ancestors: Genre originated UK late 1980s. First Seattle appearance at degree 118.
genealogy.relationship: First shoegaze. Track whether shoegaze recurs.

theory_nodes.concept: Energy descent -- E=5 (degrees 114-117, four consecutive) to E=3 (degree 118); steepest descent in recent engine history; drop of two levels; engine cooling after E=6 corridor; directed energy becomes diffuse atmosphere; shoegaze is loud but diffuse, not directed
examples: E=6 corridor at degrees 107-108. Sustained E=5 through 117. E=3 at 118.
Shoegaze E=3: loud in absolute terms but diffuse. Energy washes rather than hits.
The wall of sound is not kinetic (crashing) but potential (enveloping).
theory_nodes.description: Energy descent at degree 118. E=5 to E=3. Steepest recent drop. Engine cools.

ancestors: Four consecutive E=5 (114-117). E=6 corridor (107-108). The descent follows the peak.
genealogy.relationship: E=3 at degree 118. Track energy at 119.

cross_references.relationship: CONSECUTIVE CONTRAST. Layered guitars with hooks and clear vocals (117, E=5, Sub Pop) vs. layered guitars with submerged vocals (118, E=3, self-released). Clarity vs. dissolution.
MAXIMUM CONTRAST. Two-piece garage (116, E=5) vs. shoegaze (118, E=3). Raw vs. effected. Minimal vs. maximal. 2-minute vs. extended. Furthest poles of the sonic spectrum.
IMMERSION SPECTRUM. Motorik repetition (110, E=5) vs. textural density (118, E=3). Different mechanisms, shared destination: dissolution of the listener into the sound.
GRUNGE VS. SHOEGAZE. Nirvana helped kill shoegaze (1991). Shoegaze appears in Nirvana's city at degree 118. The city that destroyed it revives it. History folds.
POST-PUNK vs. SHOEGAZE. Angular, structured (115, E=5) vs. diffuse, atmospheric (118, E=3). Adjacent noise-informed genres, opposite approaches.
